element_introduction_order.element: Basic team selection and match setup
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns menu navigation and match configuration before first match
Learned through immediate gameplay during opening match
Introduced after player demonstrates competency with basic controls

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Sports game rhythm: discrete match cycles with build-up during play, resolution at match conclusion. Tension scales with match competitiveness and remaining time.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory difficulty with AI opponents at baseline skill. Emphasizes learning match mechanics and menu systems.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased AI competency and tactical sophistication. Opponents employ more complex formations and strategies.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Maximum difficulty settings with advanced AI tactics. Player must demonstrate mastery of all systems.
notable_spikes: Transition from tutorial/introductory matches to competitive play
Higher difficulty tiers with enhanced AI tactical behavior

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard sports game loop: pre-match preparation (team/formation selection) → live match play → post-match results. Each cycle completes one competitive unit.

Game difficulty increases through selectable difficulty settings and opponent AI enhancement rather than sequential level progression. Players encounter varied team matchups requiring tactical adaptation.

sources_used: wikipedia - International Superstar Soccer series information

extraction_notes.paradigm_clarification: Source listed paradigm as 'racing' but game is actually a football/soccer sports simulation. Corrected to 'sports' based on Wikipedia content.
extraction_notes.data_limitations: TCRF source was inaccessible/protected. Wikipedia provides release and platform information but limited design documentation. No detailed level-by-level progression data available. Analysis based on genre conventions for 1994 sports games.
extraction_notes.confidence_level: Low-to-moderate. Element introduction and rhythm patterns inferred from sports game conventions rather than primary design documentation.
